title: Install HyperFrames skills on director profile
body: Run `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes --all --full-depth` under the director profile. Verify `/hyperframes` router loads and lists creation workflows.

title: Install HyperFrames skills on reviewer profile
body: Run `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes --all --full-depth` under the reviewer profile. Confirm `npx hyperframes lint --help` works.

title: Install HyperFrames skills on animator profile
body: Run `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes --all --full-depth` under the animator profile. Read `/hyperframes-animation`, `/hyperframes-keyframes`, `/hyperframes-creative`.

title: Install HyperFrames skills on voice-engineer profile
body: Run `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes --all --full-depth` under the voice-engineer profile. Read `/media-use` for audio/TTS workflows.

title: Install HyperFrames skills on web-integrator profile
body: Run `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes --all --full-depth` under the web-integrator profile. Read `/hyperframes-cli` for render/publish.

title: Write video style guide (frame.md) for Lupine Science
body: Translate the web design system into a frame.md spec: palette, typography sizes at 1080p, safe margins, lower-third zone, transition language, logo use.

title: Define brand palette lock for video
body: Constrain all compositions to warm paper #faf9f6, indigo #3d4db3, ink #1a1a1a, amber #e8a838, sage #5a8a6e, slate #6b7c8e, rose #c75b5b. No other colors without director approval.

title: Create reusable title-card component
body: Build `compositions/title-card.html` with Lupine logo, episode title, deck, and animated entrance. Lint/validate/inspect passes.

title: Create reusable lower-third component
body: Build `compositions/lower-third.html` for narrator line captions / source callouts. Font ≥ 48 px, mono or serif as appropriate.

title: Create reusable indigo-line transition
body: Build `compositions/transition-wipe.html`: a 12-frame indigo stroke that wipes across the screen between scenes.

title: Create reusable data-chart animation component
body: Build `compositions/data-chart.html` that accepts a data JSON and animates bars/lines/points with GSAP. Document usage.

title: Create reusable logo sting / outro
body: Build `compositions/logo-sting.html` and `compositions/outro.html` for series consistency.

title: Set up shared video asset library
body: Create `media/projects/article-videos/assets/` with source images, audio beds, fonts, and a manifest.json. All assets must be 1920×1080 or vector.

title: Set up render CI check for compositions
body: Add a CI step that runs `npm run check` on every composition before the web build. Fail on lint errors.

title: Define storyboard template
body: Create a frame.md-based storyboard template with beat, narration line, visual, animation note, and duration columns.

title: Define narration script template
body: Create a script template with word count, target WPM, pronunciation notes, and visual cue columns.

title: Create video review checklist kanban card
body: Convert REVIEW_FRAMEWORK.md into a reusable checklist ticket that must be linked to every article review.

title: Train reviewer agents on the checklist
body: Run a review session on the Article 1 prototype, score every frame, and publish the annotated frames as the training example.

title: Train director agents on sign-off criteria
body: Director watches Article 1 prototype, records a reject/approve decision with rationale, and publishes the decision rubric.

title: Set up frame-extraction QA script
body: Write `scripts/extract-review-frames.sh` that takes an MP4 and outputs frames at 5-second intervals and every cue point.

title: Set up audio QA script
body: Write `scripts/audio-qa.sh` that checks narration LUFS, peak levels, and BGM ducking. Reject if narration is outside -16 to -14 LUFS.

title: Set up caption/subtitle workflow
body: Define VTT generation pipeline, spell-check step, and time-sync verification against the rendered MP4.

title: Set up HeyGen avatar / talking-head workflow
body: Research optional synthetic robot avatars via HeyGen API or Studio only if director approves an avatar experiment. Default remains Edge TTS robot voice with no human presenter. Document lip-sync pipeline and cost per minute.

title: Set up vertical-cut generation workflow
body: Document how to reframe each 16:9 composition into 9:16 for shorts/reels without cropping critical text.

title: Define file naming and version conventions
body: Publish naming scheme: `<slug>-v<major>.<minor>.mp4`, renders/<slug>/vNN/ directories, changelog per video.

title: Create project README for video team
body: Update README.md with onboarding, skill install commands, render commands, review process, and escalation path.

title: Set up CDN path for video hosting
body: Ensure `/videos/` directory scales; evaluate Cloudflare R2 if total video volume exceeds Pages asset limits.

title: Create video sitemap and schema.org VideoObject
body: Add `<link rel="alternate" type="video/mp4">` to articles and VideoObject JSON-LD for each published video.

title: Define accessibility requirements
body: Every published video must have captions (WebVTT) and a transcript. Poster images must have alt text.

title: Create final delivery spec
body: Define deliverables per video: 1080p MP4, 720p web MP4 ≤ 3 MB/min, poster JPG, VTT captions, transcript TXT.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Write narration script
body: Distill the article into a 90–120 second script for the soothing, clear technical-robot narrator. Include visual cues for each beat. Target 145–160 WPM.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Director approves narration script
body: Review script for accuracy, tone, pacing, and the soothing technical-robot voice. Approve or request revisions with line notes.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Generate TTS narration with final voice
body: Produce narration audio using the configured robot voice (Edge TTS en-US-SteffanNeural by default). Export WAV at -16 LUFS.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Mix narration with ambient bed
body: Add subtle ambient bed, duck music under narration, export final WAV and MP3. No clipping.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Produce word-level transcript
body: Generate accurate transcript with timestamps. Use as source for captions and article transcript block.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Create storyboard / beat sheet
body: Map every narration line to a visual, animation, and timing. Use the shared storyboard template.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Director approves storyboard
body: Review storyboard for narrative clarity, brand alignment, and motion ambition. Approve or reject.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Build HyperFrames composition
body: Create the root `index.html` composition, wire narration audio, register paused GSAP timeline, and pass `npm run check`.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Implement kinetic titles and lower-thirds
body: Add animated title card, section labels, and lower-thirds. Text ≥ 48 px, safe margins respected.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Implement data/chart animations
body: Animate each data visual so the insight builds on screen (bars grow, lines draw, points populate). No static charts.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Implement transitions and logo sting
body: Use the shared indigo-line transition between scenes. Add logo sting at open and outro at close.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Review composition frames against checklist
body: Extract frames every 5 seconds + cue points. Score each against REVIEW_FRAMEWORK.md. Attach flagged frames.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Flag unreadable text or off-brand frames
body: Mark any frame with text < 36 px, generic illustrations, color violations, or unclear focal points. File P0/P1 tickets.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Fix all P0/P1 frame issues
body: Re-render or redesign flagged frames. Adjust typography, color, animation, or replace assets. Re-run lint.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Re-review flagged frames
body: Verify every flagged frame is now ≥ 7/10. Close or escalate remaining issues to director.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Render final 1080p MP4
body: Run `npm run render` at 1920×1080 30fps. Verify no errors and audio sync.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Director watches final render and approves
body: Watch at full size with audio. Approve for web or reject with timestamped notes.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Compress web MP4 to ≤ 3 MB/min
body: Encode 720p web version with H.264 CRF 28 and AAC 128k. Confirm file size budget.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Add video link to article page
body: Place `/videos/<slug>.mp4` link in article header if final render is approved. Update build-articles.mjs if needed.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: Add video card to /videos/ index
body: Add poster, title, deck, and duration to public/videos/index.html. Sort by article order.

title: The 0.2% Synthesis Problem: QA smoke test and verify video link
body: Run `npm run smoke` and confirm `/videos/<slug>.mp4` returns 200 with correct content-type.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Implement kinetic titles and lower-thirds
body: Add animated title card, section labels, and lower-thirds. Text ≥ 48 px, safe margins respected.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Implement data/chart animations
body: Animate each data visual so the insight builds on screen (bars grow, lines draw, points populate). No static charts.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Implement transitions and logo sting
body: Use the shared indigo-line transition between scenes. Add logo sting at open and outro at close.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Review composition frames against checklist
body: Extract frames every 5 seconds + cue points. Score each against REVIEW_FRAMEWORK.md. Attach flagged frames.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Flag unreadable text or off-brand frames
body: Mark any frame with text < 36 px, generic illustrations, color violations, or unclear focal points. File P0/P1 tickets.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Fix all P0/P1 frame issues
body: Re-render or redesign flagged frames. Adjust typography, color, animation, or replace assets. Re-run lint.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Re-review flagged frames
body: Verify every flagged frame is now ≥ 7/10. Close or escalate remaining issues to director.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Render final 1080p MP4
body: Run `npm run render` at 1920×1080 30fps. Verify no errors and audio sync.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Director watches final render and approves
body: Watch at full size with audio. Approve for web or reject with timestamped notes.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Compress web MP4 to ≤ 3 MB/min
body: Encode 720p web version with H.264 CRF 28 and AAC 128k. Confirm file size budget.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Add video link to article page
body: Place `/videos/<slug>.mp4` link in article header if final render is approved. Update build-articles.mjs if needed.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: Add video card to /videos/ index
body: Add poster, title, deck, and duration to public/videos/index.html. Sort by article order.

title: Water and Air: Correcting the Molecules We Drink and Breathe: QA smoke test and verify video link
body: Run `npm run smoke` and confirm `/videos/<slug>.mp4` returns 200 with correct content-type.

title: Methane and Refrigerants: Cutting the Non-CO₂ Climate Forcers: Write narration script
body: Distill the article into a 90–120 second script for the soothing, clear technical-robot narrator. Include visual cues for each beat. Target 145–160 WPM.

title: Methane and Refrigerants: Cutting the Non-CO₂ Climate Forcers: Director approves narration script
body: Review script for accuracy, tone, pacing, and the soothing technical-robot voice. Approve or request revisions with line notes.

title: Methane and Refrigerants: Cutting the Non-CO₂ Climate Forcers: Generate TTS narration with final voice
body: Produce narration audio using the configured robot voice (Edge TTS en-US-SteffanNeural by default). Export WAV at -16 LUFS.

title: Methane and Refrigerants: Cutting the Non-CO₂ Climate Forcers: Mix narration with ambient bed
body: Add subtle ambient bed, duck music under narration, export final WAV and MP3. No clipping.

title: Methane and Refrigerants: Cutting the Non-CO₂ Climate Forcers: Produce word-level transcript
body: Generate accurate transcript with timestamps. Use as source for captions and article transcript block.

title: Methane and Refrigerants: Cutting the Non-CO₂ Climate Forcers: Create storyboard / beat sheet
body: Map every narration line to a visual, animation, and timing. Use the shared storyboard template.

title: Methane and Refrigerants: Cutting the Non-CO₂ Climate Forcers: Director approves storyboard
body: Review storyboard for narrative clarity, brand alignment, and motion ambition. Approve or reject.
